final_prompt.prompt:
Create a packaging design mockup for a premium organic matcha product line, featuring four SKUs arranged on a light wood surface for a high-end gift market.

Style/medium: commercial product photography composited with packaging design render; Japanese wabi-sabi minimalism meets contemporary graphic design; matte surface rendering with fine illustrative detail
Composition: four products arranged neatly side by side on a light-toned wooden tabletop, slight elevated three-quarter angle, equal visual weight per SKU, generous negative space around the group
Subject: four matcha product packages — matcha powder tin/pouch, matcha chocolate bar, matcha cookies box, and matcha tea bags box — each bearing a hand-drawn illustration of a matcha tea garden with clean, elegant linework; trilingual product name typography (Chinese / English / Japanese) set in a combination of serif and handwritten script typefaces
Scene/backdrop: light natural wood surface, scattered fresh tea leaves and loose matcha powder as accent props, soft neutral background
Lighting/mood: soft diffused natural light from upper left, minimal shadows, clean and serene atmosphere evoking refined East Asian aesthetics
Color palette: layered matcha green tones (deep forest green to pale celadon) as dominant; warm off-white/cream as base; restrained gold as accent on borders and typography details
Materials/textures: matte uncoated paper stock with subtle tactile grain; fine traditional Japanese pattern (e.g., seigaiha or asanoha) printed as a tonal background texture on each package surface
Text (verbatim): 中英日三语排版
Constraints: all four packages must be fully legible and individually distinct; gold details must read as metallic foil without gradients becoming garish; Japanese pattern must be tonal/subtle, not dominant
Avoid: glossy plastic surfaces, neon greens, cluttered composition, generic flat-lay clichés, Western candy-bar aesthetics, drop shadows that feel digital, watermarks
